mnemonics: H.A.S.P. - Heterogeneous, Asymmetric, Symmetric, Processor Affinity
keyPoints: Symmetric multiprocessing (SMP) allows any processor to handle any task, while asymmetric multiprocessing (AMP) designates specific roles like a master processor.
Processor affinity is a scheduler feature that attempts to keep a process running on the same processor to avoid the performance cost of invalidating and repopulating cache memory (cache misses).
Load balancing distributes work evenly across all processors but can conflict with processor affinity; systems often use push or pull migration techniques to manage this trade-off.
commonMistakes: Assuming more processors always lead to better performance, ignoring the overhead of synchronization and load balancing.
Confusing Symmetric (SMP) and Asymmetric (AMP) multiprocessing architectures and their scheduling implications.
